resources.string: All experiments will be permanently deleted.
Try changing the filters\\nor resetting them
Run the first experiment — \\nthe results will appear here.
Launch an experiment so it appears here
Coulomb\'s law is a physical law describing the force with which electrically charged bodies attract or repel each other. The greater the charges, the stronger the interaction.
The Doppler effect is a change in the frequency of a wave (sound, light, or a radio signal) due to the motion of the source or the observer.
The free fall of bodies is defined as motion occurring solely under the influence of the force of gravity.
Harmonic oscillations are oscillations in which a certain physical quantity varies over time according to a harmonic (sinusoidal or cosinusoidal) law.

Joule–Lenz law is a physical law that determines the amount of heat generated by a conductor when an electric current flows through it. It establishes a relationship between the thermal effect of the current and the electrical parameters of the circuit.
A mathematical pendulum is a model of a body suspended from a massless string that oscillates under the influence of gravity.

An oscillator consisting of a rigid body undergoing oscillations in a force field about a point other than the body\'s center of mass, or about a fixed axis perpendicular to the direction of the forces and not passing through the body\'s center of mass.
Motion of a body projected at an angle to the horizontal

The motion of a body projected at an angle to the horizontal is a complex motion in which the horizontal velocity component remains constant, while the vertical component changes under the influence of gravity. Projection from an initial height is supported.

Radioactive decay is the spontaneous transformation of unstable atomic nuclei, accompanied by a decrease in the number of radioactive nuclei over time. The process is described by the exponential decay law.

A spring-mass system is a mechanical oscillatory system consisting of a mass attached to an elastic spring with a specific spring constant, with the other end of the spring rigidly fixed. It undergoes oscillations about an equilibrium position under the action of an elastic restoring force.
